summary: First full e2e test of Science of Autonomy MVP. Backend: 16/16 pytest tests pass (root, tracks x3, foundations modules/5 lessons, PID lesson with quiz+socialClip+prev/next, register/login/me with Bearer, admin seed login, complete-lesson XP award + idempotency, assessment recommendedTrack mapping, social-clips per lesson, calendar.csv with Content-Disposition). Frontend: landing renders 3D drone canvas + 3 track cards + Dronability CTA; register -> /onboarding -> /tracks/{goal}; /tracks shows 3 live tracks; foundations track detail lists 5 lesson rows; /lessons/l-pid renders PID Tuner widget (P/I/D sliders + Canvas), Quiz works (CHECK 1/2, answers [0,1] mark correct); Complete lesson awards 40 XP visible in nav badge and Dashboard (xp=40, level=2); Studio shows 16 social clips and CSV download link works; Assessment Start advances to Mental Rotation 1/6.

issues: Recharts console warning: 'The width(-1) and height(-1) of chart should be greater than 0' fires during first mount of the step-response graph. Cosmetic only, chart still renders after layout settles, but the parent container should have a guaranteed min-height to silence the warning.

Two 401 errors logged to console from initial /api/auth/me probe for guests. Expected behavior, but it pollutes the console — consider suppressing the auth bootstrap fetch when no token is in localStorage.

action_items: Optional: skip the initial /api/auth/me call on the landing page when no soa_token is present in localStorage to avoid harmless 401s in the console.
Optional: give the step-response chart container an explicit min-height (e.g., 200px) to silence the Recharts width=-1/height=-1 warning on first render.
Optional: Drone3D.jsx in landing shows a deprecation warning from three.js (THREE.Clock); non-blocking.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py:104 xp_to_level uses sqrt curve — fine. Streak logic in /progress/complete always increments on a 'different day' (never resets on a gap). Per spec 'streak' should reset to 1 when there's a missed day; current implementation also increments when the user returns after a multi-day gap. Consider comparing last_active_day to today-1 to differentiate consecutive vs gapped activity.

server.py CORSMiddleware uses allow_credentials=True with default allow_origins='*' (wildcard). Browsers reject credentialed requests against a wildcard origin — works today only because the frontend uses Bearer in Authorization header, not cookies. If you intend the httpOnly auth_token cookie to actually flow, CORS_ORIGINS must list explicit origins.
server.py uses @app.on_event('startup'/'shutdown') which is deprecated in FastAPI/Starlette; migrate to lifespan context manager.
Auth cookie is set with secure=True samesite='none' but path='/' — fine for prod HTTPS; will not be set on http://localhost.

register/login return token in the body AND set httpOnly cookie — that's redundant but acceptable. The frontend currently relies on Bearer only (withCredentials=false), so the cookie is effectively unused.
test_credentials: Admin seed verified: admin@scienceofautonomy.app / autonomy2026 — login returns role=admin. Fresh test users registered as TEST_user_*/TEST_ui_*/TEST_flow_* with password TestPass123!. NOTE: /app/memory/test_credentials.md does not exist; consider adding one.
seed_data_creation: No new seed data created. Tests create TEST_-prefixed users on the fly; not cleaned up afterwards (acceptable for MVP but consider a teardown).
context_for_next_testing_agent: All MVP acceptance criteria verified end-to-end. Backend pytest suite at /app/backend/tests/test_backend_api.py is the regression baseline. PID quiz has 2 questions with answer indices [0,1]; user must answer BOTH to see lesson-complete-button. The step-response chart in PIDTuner emits a benign width=-1 warning on first mount. Two 401s from /api/auth/me on landing for guests are expected (not real bugs). Assessment flow not driven to completion in UI (Vigilance phase needs 35s real-time) — but backend /api/assessment/submit is fully tested.
rca of the issue: No blocking issues. Minor improvements suggested in critical_code_review_comments and action_items.
